question: Is Novo Nordisk's revenue decline (-7.6% YoY) temporary or structural?

findings.claim: Eli Lilly now commands 58% of US GLP-1 prescriptions vs Novo's 42% — a complete reversal from 2024 when Novo had 69%
findings.data: Market share shift: Novo held 69% global GLP-1 share in Q2 2024. By Q1 2025, Lilly overtook with 53%. By Q2 2025, Lilly had ~57%. As of late 2025, Lilly held 58% of US GLP-1 Rx vs Novo's 42%. Novo's global GLP-1 share dropped from 55.7% to 49.3% between Aug 2024 and Aug 2025. Key driver: tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) achieves ~20% weight loss vs semaglutide's ~14% in trials.

findings.claim: Lilly guided +27% revenue growth in 2026 ($80-83B) while Novo guided -5% to -13% — a 30-40 point divergence
findings.data: Lilly 2026 guidance: Revenue $80-83B, +27% growth. Mounjaro projected $22.8B, Zepbound projected $18.1B in 2026. Lilly's Q4 2025: Revenue +43% to $19.3B; Mounjaro +110% to $7.4B; Zepbound +123% to $4.3B. FY2025 combined Mounjaro+Zepbound: $36.5B (~56% of total Lilly revenue). Novo 2026: -5% to -13% CER. This is the widest growth divergence between the two companies.

findings.claim: Compounded semaglutide shipments dropped 90% after FDA removed semaglutide from shortage list in Feb 2025
findings.data: FDA removed semaglutide from drug shortage list Feb 21, 2025. Enforcement deadlines: 503A pharmacies (April 22, 2025), 503B outsourcing facilities (May 22, 2025). Shipments of compounded semaglutide dropped 90% from June 2024 to June 2025 per FDA data. However, 503A pharmacies still compound under patient-specific prescriptions. The Outsourcing Facilities Association (OFA) is suing the FDA. Chinese API suppliers pivoted to generic manufacturing for patent-expiry markets.

findings.claim: Lilly plans to launch orforglipron (oral GLP-1 for obesity) in US in Q2 2026 — a direct competitor to oral Wegovy
findings.data: Eli Lilly expects to launch orforglipron, an oral non-peptide GLP-1, for obesity in the US in Q2 2026. This would directly compete with Novo's oral Wegovy. Unlike semaglutide, orforglipron is a small molecule (not a peptide), meaning it could be cheaper to manufacture and potentially more convenient.
